item.title: Every Hitman Game, Ranked
item.description:
With 007 First Light , IO Interactive returned the greatest spy in fiction into the gaming limelight, and James Bond's journey was shaped by Agent 47 (to an extent). Over the course of two decades, Hitman cemented itself as one of the defining stealth franchises, with its sandbox approach setting it apart from other pillars like Splinter Cell . Dropped into a dense map and told to take out a target, players control Agent 47 as they plan, stalk, and disguise their way to a silent assassination. This formula simply works.

IO Interactive has created more than 10 Hitman games, and they are certainly not all equal. Nearly every project has enough positives to warrant a recommendation, but some of them fall short in ways that make them harder to revisit. Still, at its peak, Hitman might just be the best stealth series of all time, especially in terms of gameplay. Let's rank all the Hitman games.

Updated June 12, 2026, by Mark Sammut: I updated the images, along with rewriting the Codename 47 and Absolution sections. I also removed the compilations as they aren't really necessary.

Quick Picks: Best Hitman Games To Play Right Now

Before we dive into the individual games, we have put together a short section that provides quick recommendations across a range of categories. If you are just looking for something to play right now, or are new to the franchise and don't know where to start, the following table should help.

Category

Game

Reason

Best Hitman Game For Beginners

Hitman: World of Assassination

In 2026, World of Assassination is an absolute no-brainer, as the compilation comes with a ridiculous amount of content. They are also the most polished Hitman games by quite a margin.

Best Hitman Game (Not Counting Compilations)

Hitman 3

Perfects the formula started by its two immediate predecessors, which refined Hitman to a point of near-perfection.

Best Classic Hitman Game

Hitman: Blood Money

Although the first three games have their merits, Blood Money is the oldest game that has aged well; even in 2006, it was far ahead of its predecessors.

Best Hitman Action Game

Hitman: Absolution

Guns blazing should be the last resort in a Hitman game, but Absolution encourages you to go down that route a bit more than the rest of the series.

Best Hitman Mobile Game

Hitman: Blood Money - Reprisal

A solid adaptation of Blood Money that gives you a console Hitman experience on the go. If you want a game made specifically for mobile devices, Hitman Go is the best.

14 Hitman: Codename 47

The First Entry In The Hitman Series Is A Rough But Solid Effort

Hitman: Codename 47
Released
November 19, 2000

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

Developer(s)
IO Interactive
Platform(s)
PC
Genre(s)
Stealth
Powered by

Hitman: Codename 47 is the first game in the series, and it laid the groundwork for what would become a great series, introducing several mechanics like taking and using disguises, the concealed sniper rifle, and the need for stealth. Just like its newest titles, the game rewards the most cost-effective methods to complete a mission and gives penalties for killing civilians or getting spotted by witnesses.

Unfortunately, many of these mechanics are antiquated by modern standards, making the game feel unpolished. Codename 47 's importance cannot be understated, but it wasn't a particularly fantastic game for its era either, and it hasn't gotten better with age. Most of the worst Hitman levels are in Codename 47 , and they aren't helped by janky controls and a poorly thought-out save system. IO Interactive never released the game on another platform aside from the PC, opting instead to kind of retell it in Hitman Contracts , and that was probably for the best.

13 Hitman: Sniper

A Mobile Spin-Off From Hitman 2's Assassin Mode

Hitman: Sniper
First-Person Shooter
Stealth
Systems
Released
June 4, 2015
ESRB
m
Developer(s)
Square Enix Montreal

Platform(s)
Android, iOS
Genre(s)
First-Person Shooter, Stealth
Powered by

Hitman: Sniper was released in 2015 for iOS and Android systems. It is a spin-off that works similarly to the Hitman Sniper Assassin mode in Hitman 2 and the stand-alone Sniper Challenge mode for Hitman: Absolution.

Hitman: Sniper plays like a gallery shooter with several objectives for the player to complete their mission. The player can create openings by setting up traps, using distractions, and other mechanics that Hitman fans will enjoy. However, the touchscreen controls are often at odds with the zoom function, and players will be frustrated by accidentally shooting their guns when trying to use the scope.

12 Hitman 2: Sniper Assassin

The Sniper Assassin Mode From Hitman 2

Hitman 2
8 /10
Released
November 13, 2018

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

Developer(s)
IO Interactive
Platform(s)
Xbox One, PS4, PC
Genre(s)
Stealth, Action
Powered by

The Sniper Assassin mode included in Hitman 2 is now part of the Legacy pack in Hitman 3 , and the package consists of separate missions that place Agent 47 at a single vantage point using only a sniper rifle. While that may sound simplistic on paper, the levels retain their sandbox feel and allow 47 to cause as much chaos and distractions as possible.

In addition to the main objectives and targets, there are optional challenges like causing environmental accidents and shooting suspended objects, such as chandeliers or heavy wall-mounted speakers to fall on a target, killing them instantly. Making these hits look like an accident can lead to hilarious and memorable moments.

11 Hitman 2: Sniper Assassin – Knight And Stone

Co-Op Gameplay With Two New Characters In The Hitman Universe

The Knight and Stone missions were part of Hitman 2’s standalone online co-op gameplay missions. Up to three players could take part in sniper missions where players take on the roles of Agent 47, Knight, and Stone.

Like the Sniper Assassin missions, the characters remain in place, but the environments are still sandboxes that can be manipulated. The missions offer bonuses for synchronized kills, and the new characters, Knight and Stone, are far more talkative than 47, offering players a new dynamic. Unfortunately, these missions are no longer available, as the developers focus on the single-player aspects of Hitman 3 instead, but fans are hopeful that it will make a comeback.

Build your perfect top ten one reveal at a time.

—

—

Your Ranking

—

0 / 10
Pick a spot for this card.

Final Ranking

—

Your blind ranking is locked in.

More Games

Swap two picks?

Pick two spots to swap before locking in your final ranking.

Build your perfect top ten one reveal at a time.

—

—
Your Ranking —
0 / 10
Pick a spot for this card.
Final Ranking
—
Your blind ranking is locked in.
More Games
Swap two picks? Pick two spots to swap before locking in your final ranking.

10 Hitman 3 VR

Immersive VR Action Through The Eyes Of Agent 47

On the PlayStation 4 – or the PlayStation 5 via backward compatibility – Hitman 3 also comes with a fantastic VR mode, which is currently exclusive to the PSVR. This mode allows players to play the game through the eyes of Agent 47 and is incredibly immersive. Thanks to the VR mode, players now seem to have a new line of sight that allows 47 to observe the environment in brand-new ways. Moreover, it is one of the best-looking games on the PSVR, which is surprising considering the size and scale of the missions.

The Hitman VR mode is now available on the PC as part of Hitman 3 's year 2 plan. Unfortunately, as a direct port of the PSVR version, it lacks any enhancements that many PC players would have expected, and it's a choppy experience by comparison, impacting half of its audience. Hitman 3 VR: Reloaded was released for the Meta Quest 3, but it had bugs and gameplay issues.

9 Hitman: Contracts

A Darker Sequel And A Retelling Of Some Of Codename 47's Most Important Missions

Hitman: Contracts
Released
April 20, 2004

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

Developer(s)
IO Interactive
Platform(s)
PC, PS2, PS3, Xbox (Original), Xbox 360
Genre(s)
Third-Person Shooter, Stealth
Powered by

Hitman: Contracts ' story is told in the past tense, allowing players to catch up with the first game's events without having to endure playing it. Contracts featured many retold and reworked missions from the original game, retold through the memories of a mortally injured Agent 47. Hitman: Contracts makes each mission feel like a self-contained story with world-building and unique characters that draw fans into the hit as they calculate the best methods to take down their targets.

In addition, Contracts is much darker and more violent in tone than its predecessors, which is immediately evident in its opening level at Meat King's nightclub.

The globe-trotting Contracts take players to locations like Romania, Siberia, the UK, Rotterdam, Budapest, Hong Kong, and Paris. The Paris Mission features an action-packed epilogue, seemingly inspired by the movie Leon: The Professional. It's another entry in the series that holds up well against the new titles and is among the series' darkest in its story and tone. Hitman: Contracts was remastered for the PS3 and Xbox 360 as part of the Hitman HD Trilogy, but is unavailable on modern consoles. Hopefully, IO will revisit this classic and make it available to new fans.

8 Hitman: Absolution

The Black Sheep Of The Hitman Franchise

Hitman: Absolution
8 /10
Released
November 20, 2012

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

Developer(s)
IO Interactive, Nixxes
Platform(s)
PC, PS3, Xbox 360
Genre(s)
Stealth, Third-Person Shooter
Powered by

IO Interactive has its share of divisive games, but nothing else comes close to Hitman: Absolution . From the very start, Hitman thrived in crafting open-ended levels that effectively serve as puzzles in a sandbox. The series emphasizes player freedom, dense environments, and open-ended assassinations, and Blood Money exemplified this formula. Hitman: Absolution did away with almost everything that defined the franchise, to the point of feeling like a soft reboot rather than a proper sequel. Crucially, the game sold well enough to cause fans to fear that IO Interactive might continue down this direction. In 2026, we know that didn't end up being the case, which makes this entry a bit easier to enjoy as a strange detour that only kind of works.

Except for a handful of levels that echo other entries, Hitman: Absolution sits alone as an action-first journey that only intermittently encourages creative stealth. Most stages are incredibly linear, essentially following corridors that provide little room for experimentation. You can basically play Absolution as a cover shooter, and you might honestly have more fun that way. The story is, well, awful, and mishandles Agent 47, turning the stoic super assassin into a bit of a bumbling idiot. The disguise system was also broken, to the point that an Instinct meter was created to paper over the cracks.

If you don't go in expecting a Hitman game, Absolution 's silliness and over-the-top nature can be entertaining, and it plays relatively well despite its not-so-great mechanics. If you go in expecting Hitman , Absolution is horrendous.

7 Hitman Go

A Surprisingly Good Puzzle Stealth Game

Hitman Go
Puzzle
Strategy
Systems
Released
April 17, 2014
ESRB
e
Developer(s)
Square Enix Montreal

Platform(s)
Android, iOS, PC, PS4, PS Vita
Genre(s)
Puzzle, Strategy
Powered by

Hitman Go launched on Android and iOS mobile systems in 2014, but it was later released on the PlayStation 4 and Vita in 2016. Instead of a third-person perspective featured in the mainline series, Hitman Go is a turn-based, top-down game where Agent 47 moves around using grids.

Despite resembling a board game, this mobile entry captured the puzzle-like mechanics of the mainline series, albeit simplified to better fit mobile devices. Elements of the series are missing, like completing missions without killing non-targets, and the restrictive nature of Hitman Go has inescapable linearity. It scores well, but doesn't feel like a proper entry in the Hitman series.

6 Hitman 2: Silent Assassin

An Impressive Title and a Significant Improvement Over Its Predecessor

Hitman 2: Silent Assassin
Stealth
Systems
Released
October 1, 2002

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

Developer(s)
IO Interactive
Platform(s)
GameCube, PC, PS2, PS3, Xbox (Original), Xbox 360
Genre(s)
Stealth
Powered by

Hitman 2: Silent Assassin was an authentic sandbox experience that became the standard for the series going forward. It's also available as an HD release in the Hitman Trilogy set for the PS3, the Xbox 360, and the Hitman HD Enhanced Collection. Where the first game in the series felt like a rough draft or experimentation of ideas, its direct sequel, Hitman 2: Silent Assassin, polished everything for it to become the series fans love today.

Using disguises and hiding in plain sight played a big part in being as stealthy as possible. Additionally, Hitman 2 has a variety of ways to eliminate targets and achieve the Silent Assassin grade, from poisoning drinks, setting up traps, or sniping from a distance. Players can even run in and use an aggressive approach, but the reward comes in being the assassin Agent 47 is supposed to be. The original Hitman 2 is one of the highest-scoring games in the series, but its age and unrefined mechanics compared to its successors make it clunkier and more challenging than the latest entries.

5 Hitman: Blood Money

One Of The Best Stealth Games Of All Time

Hitman: Blood Money
Released
May 30, 2006

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

Developer(s)
IO Interactive
Platform(s)
Android, iOS, PC, PS2, PS3, Switch, Xbox (Original), Xbox 360
Genre(s)
Third-Person Shooter, Stealth
Powered by

Hitman: Blood Money is often celebrated as a favorite among fans. Hitman: Blood Money features the most compelling story in the series and is told through interview segments between a journalist and the former head of the FBI. The sandbox-level design was more flexible than its sequel, Absolution. The game features some of the most varied and the series's most memorable missions. In addition, enemy AI saw significant improvements, and the newly introduced notoriety system added to the tense atmosphere.

Missions variety and immersion are ramped up, with outstanding levels like 'A New Life and A Dance With The Devil' being standouts. Hitman: Blood Money was repackaged with Hitman Absolution and enhanced in 4K for the PS4 and Xbox in 2019, and this pack is the way to go if you can find it. Blood Money: Reprisal was launched in 2024 for Android, iOS, and the Switch and holds up well in handheld form.
item.content:encoded:
With 007 First Light , IO Interactive returned the greatest spy in fiction into the gaming limelight, and James Bond's journey was shaped by Agent 47 (to an extent). Over the course of two decades, Hitman cemented itself as one of the defining stealth franchises, with its sandbox approach setting it apart from other pillars like Splinter Cell . Dropped into a dense map and told to take out a target, players control Agent 47 as they plan, stalk, and disguise their way to a silent assassination. This formula simply works.

IO Interactive has created more than 10 Hitman games, and they are certainly not all equal. Nearly every project has enough positives to warrant a recommendation, but some of them fall short in ways that make them harder to revisit. Still, at its peak, Hitman might just be the best stealth series of all time, especially in terms of gameplay. Let's rank all the Hitman games.

Updated June 12, 2026, by Mark Sammut: I updated the images, along with rewriting the Codename 47 and Absolution sections. I also removed the compilations as they aren't really necessary.

Quick Picks: Best Hitman Games To Play Right Now

Before we dive into the individual games, we have put together a short section that provides quick recommendations across a range of categories. If you are just looking for something to play right now, or are new to the franchise and don't know where to start, the following table should help.

Category

Game

Reason

Best Hitman Game For Beginners

Hitman: World of Assassination

In 2026, World of Assassination is an absolute no-brainer, as the compilation comes with a ridiculous amount of content. They are also the most polished Hitman games by quite a margin.

Best Hitman Game (Not Counting Compilations)

Hitman 3

Perfects the formula started by its two immediate predecessors, which refined Hitman to a point of near-perfection.

Best Classic Hitman Game

Hitman: Blood Money

Although the first three games have their merits, Blood Money is the oldest game that has aged well; even in 2006, it was far ahead of its predecessors.

Best Hitman Action Game

Hitman: Absolution

Guns blazing should be the last resort in a Hitman game, but Absolution encourages you to go down that route a bit more than the rest of the series.

Best Hitman Mobile Game

Hitman: Blood Money - Reprisal

A solid adaptation of Blood Money that gives you a console Hitman experience on the go. If you want a game made specifically for mobile devices, Hitman Go is the best.

14 Hitman: Codename 47

The First Entry In The Hitman Series Is A Rough But Solid Effort

Hitman: Codename 47
Released
November 19, 2000

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

Developer(s)
IO Interactive
Platform(s)
PC
Genre(s)
Stealth
Powered by

Hitman: Codename 47 is the first game in the series, and it laid the groundwork for what would become a great series, introducing several mechanics like taking and using disguises, the concealed sniper rifle, and the need for stealth. Just like its newest titles, the game rewards the most cost-effective methods to complete a mission and gives penalties for killing civilians or getting spotted by witnesses.

Unfortunately, many of these mechanics are antiquated by modern standards, making the game feel unpolished. Codename 47 's importance cannot be understated, but it wasn't a particularly fantastic game for its era either, and it hasn't gotten better with age. Most of the worst Hitman levels are in Codename 47 , and they aren't helped by janky controls and a poorly thought-out save system. IO Interactive never released the game on another platform aside from the PC, opting instead to kind of retell it in Hitman Contracts , and that was probably for the best.

13 Hitman: Sniper

A Mobile Spin-Off From Hitman 2's Assassin Mode

Hitman: Sniper
First-Person Shooter
Stealth
Systems
Released
June 4, 2015
ESRB
m
Developer(s)
Square Enix Montreal

Platform(s)
Android, iOS
Genre(s)
First-Person Shooter, Stealth
Powered by

Hitman: Sniper was released in 2015 for iOS and Android systems. It is a spin-off that works similarly to the Hitman Sniper Assassin mode in Hitman 2 and the stand-alone Sniper Challenge mode for Hitman: Absolution.

Hitman: Sniper plays like a gallery shooter with several objectives for the player to complete their mission. The player can create openings by setting up traps, using distractions, and other mechanics that Hitman fans will enjoy. However, the touchscreen controls are often at odds with the zoom function, and players will be frustrated by accidentally shooting their guns when trying to use the scope.

12 Hitman 2: Sniper Assassin

The Sniper Assassin Mode From Hitman 2

Hitman 2
8 /10
Released
November 13, 2018

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

Developer(s)
IO Interactive
Platform(s)
Xbox One, PS4, PC
Genre(s)
Stealth, Action
Powered by

The Sniper Assassin mode included in Hitman 2 is now part of the Legacy pack in Hitman 3 , and the package consists of separate missions that place Agent 47 at a single vantage point using only a sniper rifle. While that may sound simplistic on paper, the levels retain their sandbox feel and allow 47 to cause as much chaos and distractions as possible.

In addition to the main objectives and targets, there are optional challenges like causing environmental accidents and shooting suspended objects, such as chandeliers or heavy wall-mounted speakers to fall on a target, killing them instantly. Making these hits look like an accident can lead to hilarious and memorable moments.

11 Hitman 2: Sniper Assassin – Knight And Stone

Co-Op Gameplay With Two New Characters In The Hitman Universe

The Knight and Stone missions were part of Hitman 2’s standalone online co-op gameplay missions. Up to three players could take part in sniper missions where players take on the roles of Agent 47, Knight, and Stone.

Like the Sniper Assassin missions, the characters remain in place, but the environments are still sandboxes that can be manipulated. The missions offer bonuses for synchronized kills, and the new characters, Knight and Stone, are far more talkative than 47, offering players a new dynamic. Unfortunately, these missions are no longer available, as the developers focus on the single-player aspects of Hitman 3 instead, but fans are hopeful that it will make a comeback.

Build your perfect top ten one reveal at a time.

—

—

Your Ranking

—

0 / 10
Pick a spot for this card.

Final Ranking

—

Your blind ranking is locked in.

More Games

Swap two picks?

Pick two spots to swap before locking in your final ranking.

Build your perfect top ten one reveal at a time.

—

—
Your Ranking —
0 / 10
Pick a spot for this card.
Final Ranking
—
Your blind ranking is locked in.
More Games
Swap two picks? Pick two spots to swap before locking in your final ranking.

10 Hitman 3 VR

Immersive VR Action Through The Eyes Of Agent 47

On the PlayStation 4 – or the PlayStation 5 via backward compatibility – Hitman 3 also comes with a fantastic VR mode, which is currently exclusive to the PSVR. This mode allows players to play the game through the eyes of Agent 47 and is incredibly immersive. Thanks to the VR mode, players now seem to have a new line of sight that allows 47 to observe the environment in brand-new ways. Moreover, it is one of the best-looking games on the PSVR, which is surprising considering the size and scale of the missions.

The Hitman VR mode is now available on the PC as part of Hitman 3 's year 2 plan. Unfortunately, as a direct port of the PSVR version, it lacks any enhancements that many PC players would have expected, and it's a choppy experience by comparison, impacting half of its audience. Hitman 3 VR: Reloaded was released for the Meta Quest 3, but it had bugs and gameplay issues.

9 Hitman: Contracts

A Darker Sequel And A Retelling Of Some Of Codename 47's Most Important Missions

Hitman: Contracts
Released
April 20, 2004

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

Developer(s)
IO Interactive
Platform(s)
PC, PS2, PS3, Xbox (Original), Xbox 360
Genre(s)
Third-Person Shooter, Stealth
Powered by

Hitman: Contracts ' story is told in the past tense, allowing players to catch up with the first game's events without having to endure playing it. Contracts featured many retold and reworked missions from the original game, retold through the memories of a mortally injured Agent 47. Hitman: Contracts makes each mission feel like a self-contained story with world-building and unique characters that draw fans into the hit as they calculate the best methods to take down their targets.

In addition, Contracts is much darker and more violent in tone than its predecessors, which is immediately evident in its opening level at Meat King's nightclub.

The globe-trotting Contracts take players to locations like Romania, Siberia, the UK, Rotterdam, Budapest, Hong Kong, and Paris. The Paris Mission features an action-packed epilogue, seemingly inspired by the movie Leon: The Professional. It's another entry in the series that holds up well against the new titles and is among the series' darkest in its story and tone. Hitman: Contracts was remastered for the PS3 and Xbox 360 as part of the Hitman HD Trilogy, but is unavailable on modern consoles. Hopefully, IO will revisit this classic and make it available to new fans.

8 Hitman: Absolution

The Black Sheep Of The Hitman Franchise

Hitman: Absolution
8 /10
Released
November 20, 2012

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

Developer(s)
IO Interactive, Nixxes
Platform(s)
PC, PS3, Xbox 360
Genre(s)
Stealth, Third-Person Shooter
Powered by

IO Interactive has its share of divisive games, but nothing else comes close to Hitman: Absolution . From the very start, Hitman thrived in crafting open-ended levels that effectively serve as puzzles in a sandbox. The series emphasizes player freedom, dense environments, and open-ended assassinations, and Blood Money exemplified this formula. Hitman: Absolution did away with almost everything that defined the franchise, to the point of feeling like a soft reboot rather than a proper sequel. Crucially, the game sold well enough to cause fans to fear that IO Interactive might continue down this direction. In 2026, we know that didn't end up being the case, which makes this entry a bit easier to enjoy as a strange detour that only kind of works.

Except for a handful of levels that echo other entries, Hitman: Absolution sits alone as an action-first journey that only intermittently encourages creative stealth. Most stages are incredibly linear, essentially following corridors that provide little room for experimentation. You can basically play Absolution as a cover shooter, and you might honestly have more fun that way. The story is, well, awful, and mishandles Agent 47, turning the stoic super assassin into a bit of a bumbling idiot. The disguise system was also broken, to the point that an Instinct meter was created to paper over the cracks.

If you don't go in expecting a Hitman game, Absolution 's silliness and over-the-top nature can be entertaining, and it plays relatively well despite its not-so-great mechanics. If you go in expecting Hitman , Absolution is horrendous.

7 Hitman Go

A Surprisingly Good Puzzle Stealth Game

Hitman Go
Puzzle
Strategy
Systems
Released
April 17, 2014
ESRB
e
Developer(s)
Square Enix Montreal

Platform(s)
Android, iOS, PC, PS4, PS Vita
Genre(s)
Puzzle, Strategy
Powered by

Hitman Go launched on Android and iOS mobile systems in 2014, but it was later released on the PlayStation 4 and Vita in 2016. Instead of a third-person perspective featured in the mainline series, Hitman Go is a turn-based, top-down game where Agent 47 moves around using grids.

Despite resembling a board game, this mobile entry captured the puzzle-like mechanics of the mainline series, albeit simplified to better fit mobile devices. Elements of the series are missing, like completing missions without killing non-targets, and the restrictive nature of Hitman Go has inescapable linearity. It scores well, but doesn't feel like a proper entry in the Hitman series.

6 Hitman 2: Silent Assassin

An Impressive Title and a Significant Improvement Over Its Predecessor

Hitman 2: Silent Assassin
Stealth
Systems
Released
October 1, 2002

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

Developer(s)
IO Interactive
Platform(s)
GameCube, PC, PS2, PS3, Xbox (Original), Xbox 360
Genre(s)
Stealth
Powered by

Hitman 2: Silent Assassin was an authentic sandbox experience that became the standard for the series going forward. It's also available as an HD release in the Hitman Trilogy set for the PS3, the Xbox 360, and the Hitman HD Enhanced Collection. Where the first game in the series felt like a rough draft or experimentation of ideas, its direct sequel, Hitman 2: Silent Assassin, polished everything for it to become the series fans love today.

Using disguises and hiding in plain sight played a big part in being as stealthy as possible. Additionally, Hitman 2 has a variety of ways to eliminate targets and achieve the Silent Assassin grade, from poisoning drinks, setting up traps, or sniping from a distance. Players can even run in and use an aggressive approach, but the reward comes in being the assassin Agent 47 is supposed to be. The original Hitman 2 is one of the highest-scoring games in the series, but its age and unrefined mechanics compared to its successors make it clunkier and more challenging than the latest entries.

5 Hitman: Blood Money

One Of The Best Stealth Games Of All Time

Hitman: Blood Money
Released
May 30, 2006

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

Developer(s)
IO Interactive
Platform(s)
Android, iOS, PC, PS2, PS3, Switch, Xbox (Original), Xbox 360
Genre(s)
Third-Person Shooter, Stealth
Powered by

Hitman: Blood Money is often celebrated as a favorite among fans. Hitman: Blood Money features the most compelling story in the series and is told through interview segments between a journalist and the former head of the FBI. The sandbox-level design was more flexible than its sequel, Absolution. The game features some of the most varied and the series's most memorable missions. In addition, enemy AI saw significant improvements, and the newly introduced notoriety system added to the tense atmosphere.

Missions variety and immersion are ramped up, with outstanding levels like 'A New Life and A Dance With The Devil' being standouts. Hitman: Blood Money was repackaged with Hitman Absolution and enhanced in 4K for the PS4 and Xbox in 2019, and this pack is the way to go if you can find it. Blood Money: Reprisal was launched in 2024 for Android, iOS, and the Switch and holds up well in handheld form.

item.title: How to Kill an Enemy Affected by Tactical or Non-Lethal Scorestreak in Black Ops 7
item.description:
Call of Duty: Black Ops 7 has a bunch of challenges that players will need to complete, some of which are needed to earn those sought-after Special and Mastery Camos, whilst others are reserved for limited-time events. While a lot of these challenges are pretty simple and self-explanatory, others are a little harder. For those stuck on the 'kill an enemy affected by your Tactical or Non-Lethal Scorestreak' challenge , here is the best method to complete it fast in Black Ops 7 .

How to Kill an Enemy Affected by Tactical or Non-Lethal Scorestreak in Black Ops 7

Best Method & Loadout

Often in Black Ops 7 , players will get a challenge to kill an enemy affected by their Tactical or Non-Lethal Scorestreak. The easiest way to get the challenge done is to use a combination of the two. Before you go into your games, we recommend creating a loadout with the following:

  • The weapon needed for the challenge
  • Stun Grenades with the Longer Stun Overclock
  • The Bank Roll Perk & Scavenger (you can also equip one Green Perk for the Tactician Combat Specialty)
  • The following Scorestreaks: Scout Pulse, UAV, and Counter UAV with the Dispatcher Overclock equipped

With the above setup, you are set to earn your Scorestreaks faster, increase the amount of time players are stunned by your Tacticals, and also be able to resupply used Tacticals from enemies. Now, what you will want to do is jump into a match and stun your opponents before finishing them off with any weapon you need to get the challenge done. For those doing the Illicit Cargo Event, this will need to be Assault Rifles.

Once you have earned any one of your Scorestreaks, any enemies revealed by them will also count towards getting the challenge done , since all three count as Non-Lethal Scorestreaks. Because you have Bank Roll and Dispatcher equipped, earning these Scorestreaks should be relatively quick. Rinse and repeat this process to get the challenge wrapped up in no time.

For the best game mode for getting these kills fast, we recommend sticking to Nuketown 24/7 , which should yield the most kills in the shortest time. You should avoid modes like Face Off, since Scorestreaks are disabled. Hardcore Playlists can be good for skilled players, especially when using the Scorestreaks we suggested above.

Call of Duty: Black Ops 7
Released
November 14, 2025

WHERE TO PLAY

SUBSCRIPTION
DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

ESRB
Mature 17+ / Blood and Gore, Intense Violence, Strong Language, Suggestive Themes, Use of Drugs
Developer(s)
Treyarch, Raven Software
Franchise
Call of Duty, Call of Duty: Black Ops
Genre(s)
Action, FPS, Sci-Fi
Platform(s)
PC, PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X, Xbox Series S
OpenCritic Rating
Weak
Powered by
item.content:encoded:
Call of Duty: Black Ops 7 has a bunch of challenges that players will need to complete, some of which are needed to earn those sought-after Special and Mastery Camos, whilst others are reserved for limited-time events. While a lot of these challenges are pretty simple and self-explanatory, others are a little harder. For those stuck on the 'kill an enemy affected by your Tactical or Non-Lethal Scorestreak' challenge , here is the best method to complete it fast in Black Ops 7 .

How to Kill an Enemy Affected by Tactical or Non-Lethal Scorestreak in Black Ops 7

Best Method & Loadout

Often in Black Ops 7 , players will get a challenge to kill an enemy affected by their Tactical or Non-Lethal Scorestreak. The easiest way to get the challenge done is to use a combination of the two. Before you go into your games, we recommend creating a loadout with the following:

  • The weapon needed for the challenge
  • Stun Grenades with the Longer Stun Overclock
  • The Bank Roll Perk & Scavenger (you can also equip one Green Perk for the Tactician Combat Specialty)
  • The following Scorestreaks: Scout Pulse, UAV, and Counter UAV with the Dispatcher Overclock equipped

With the above setup, you are set to earn your Scorestreaks faster, increase the amount of time players are stunned by your Tacticals, and also be able to resupply used Tacticals from enemies. Now, what you will want to do is jump into a match and stun your opponents before finishing them off with any weapon you need to get the challenge done. For those doing the Illicit Cargo Event, this will need to be Assault Rifles.

Once you have earned any one of your Scorestreaks, any enemies revealed by them will also count towards getting the challenge done , since all three count as Non-Lethal Scorestreaks. Because you have Bank Roll and Dispatcher equipped, earning these Scorestreaks should be relatively quick. Rinse and repeat this process to get the challenge wrapped up in no time.

For the best game mode for getting these kills fast, we recommend sticking to Nuketown 24/7 , which should yield the most kills in the shortest time. You should avoid modes like Face Off, since Scorestreaks are disabled. Hardcore Playlists can be good for skilled players, especially when using the Scorestreaks we suggested above.

Call of Duty: Black Ops 7
Released
November 14, 2025

WHERE TO PLAY

SUBSCRIPTION
DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

ESRB
Mature 17+ / Blood and Gore, Intense Violence, Strong Language, Suggestive Themes, Use of Drugs
Developer(s)
Treyarch, Raven Software
Franchise
Call of Duty, Call of Duty: Black Ops
Genre(s)
Action, FPS, Sci-Fi
Platform(s)
PC, PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X, Xbox Series S
OpenCritic Rating
Weak
Powered by

item.title: Xbox Releases Game-Changing Console Update You Can Download Now
item.description:
Microsoft has released a very important update for Xbox Series X/S consoles that introduces the redesigned startup animation, alongside some other fun features for players. The update highlights Microsoft's continued focus on improving both the Xbox ecosystem, including Xbox Game Pass, and the experience surrounding its flagship games.

In mid-May, CEO Asha Sharma announced the rollout of the new Xbox console bootup screen, but many people were confused when it didn’t show up for them right away. That’s because it initially rolled out for participants in the Xbox Insider Program, which gives early access to system updates and new features before they are widely released. Insiders have an impact on the Xbox gaming experience by providing feedback, completing surveys, reporting issues, and even joining in on playtests. And, in this case, they got a sneak peek at the new console startup animation.

Xbox Series X/S New Startup Animation Is Now Available to Everybody

On June 10, Microsoft began rolling out the update that would bring the revamped bootup animation and sound to Xbox Series X/S consoles, among other things. Players will be able to view the new screen whenever they power on their consoles while using Shutdown (energy saving) mode. The update also introduces new Gamerscore milestone badges that evolve as players increase their score, with badge tiers ranging from 1,000 to 10,000,000.

Another feature added in the update include new library filters that make it easier to distinguish owned games from shared ones. It should also make it simpler to spot titles that are owned, installed, or no longer accessible, such as those formerly on Xbox Game Pass. Furthermore, games that are not compatible with the specific device will not be displayed. For example, Kinect games will only show up on Xbox One consoles that support the Kinect.

The update also introduces a redesigned Rewards experience that is accessible directly from the Home screen, as well as sharper Gamerpics and game artwork for Xbox Series X users running their consoles in 4K resolution. Microsoft also implemented several bug fixes that were affecting game installations, update queues, and dashboard functionality.

Guess the games from the emojis.

Gamoji

Guess the game from the emojis.

Time Left: 00:00

Title

—

Clues

—

Results

0 POINTS
—
More Games

Answers

Title

—

—

Clues

—

—

Guess the games from the emojis.
Easy (120s)Medium (90s)Hard (60s)

Gamoji

Guess the game from the emojis.
Time Left: 00:00
Title
—
Clues
—
Results
0 POINTS
—
More Games
Answers
Title
—
—
Clues
—
—

On June 12, Microsoft released another update, this one targeting Forza Horizon 6 , the latest entry in Playground Games' open-world racing franchise. Shortly after the game launched on May 19 for Xbox Series X/S and PC, players began experiencing problems with their saves becoming corrupted or disappearing entirely. Despite this, the game has smashed franchise records, with Forza Horizon 6 more than doubling FH5 's peak Steam concurrent player count during its early access period. Nevertheless, the save issue was a major one that greatly hampered players' experiences with the title, and the new console update should fix the problem.
item.content:encoded:
Microsoft has released a very important update for Xbox Series X/S consoles that introduces the redesigned startup animation, alongside some other fun features for players. The update highlights Microsoft's continued focus on improving both the Xbox ecosystem, including Xbox Game Pass, and the experience surrounding its flagship games.

In mid-May, CEO Asha Sharma announced the rollout of the new Xbox console bootup screen, but many people were confused when it didn’t show up for them right away. That’s because it initially rolled out for participants in the Xbox Insider Program, which gives early access to system updates and new features before they are widely released. Insiders have an impact on the Xbox gaming experience by providing feedback, completing surveys, reporting issues, and even joining in on playtests. And, in this case, they got a sneak peek at the new console startup animation.

Xbox Series X/S New Startup Animation Is Now Available to Everybody

On June 10, Microsoft began rolling out the update that would bring the revamped bootup animation and sound to Xbox Series X/S consoles, among other things. Players will be able to view the new screen whenever they power on their consoles while using Shutdown (energy saving) mode. The update also introduces new Gamerscore milestone badges that evolve as players increase their score, with badge tiers ranging from 1,000 to 10,000,000.

Another feature added in the update include new library filters that make it easier to distinguish owned games from shared ones. It should also make it simpler to spot titles that are owned, installed, or no longer accessible, such as those formerly on Xbox Game Pass. Furthermore, games that are not compatible with the specific device will not be displayed. For example, Kinect games will only show up on Xbox One consoles that support the Kinect.

The update also introduces a redesigned Rewards experience that is accessible directly from the Home screen, as well as sharper Gamerpics and game artwork for Xbox Series X users running their consoles in 4K resolution. Microsoft also implemented several bug fixes that were affecting game installations, update queues, and dashboard functionality.

Guess the games from the emojis.

Gamoji

On June 12, Microsoft released another update, this one targeting Forza Horizon 6 , the latest entry in Playground Games' open-world racing franchise. Shortly after the game launched on May 19 for Xbox Series X/S and PC, players began experiencing problems with their saves becoming corrupted or disappearing entirely. Despite this, the game has smashed franchise records, with Forza Horizon 6 more than doubling FH5 's peak Steam concurrent player count during its early access period. Nevertheless, the save issue was a major one that greatly hampered players' experiences with the title, and the new console update should fix the problem.

item.title: Clair Obscur Expedition 33: Best Verso Weapons, Ranked
item.description:
In Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 , players can obtain dozens of powerful weapons for each of the main characters. These weapons all have their own types of damage, scaling, as well as unique perks granted upon reaching level 4, level 10, and level 20 with each weapon. The game's unique approach to narrative allows players to access similar weapons via either Gustave or Verso, so both characters essentially share the same arsenal.

However, Verso offers his own unique gameplay style, somewhat similar to Devil May Cry games. The power of the character's skills and attacks grows as Verso reaches Perfection Ranks (from D to S) by attacking enemies and avoiding damage. When choosing the best weapon for Verso in Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 , players have to keep in mind this one-of-a-kind dynamic. Reaching higher tiers like A or S drastically increases all of Verso's damage (up to +200%) while also unlocking additional bonuses for Verso's skills. These are the best weapons for Verso in Expedition 33 .

Updated June 12, 2026, by Vlad Mazanko: With its robust and flexible combat system, rich itemization, and wealth of stats and perks that contribute to success in battle, Clair Obscur is a prime example of an RPG that rewards experimentation, creativity, and skill. Every player can easily find a primary weapon for Verso among the 29 total weapons available that complements their unique playstyle or very specific combat build, unlocking strong synergies with his skills and Perfection mechanics. The list has been updated to highlight the strongest weapons for Verso in Expedition 33, including those added in the Thank You Update .

17 Seeram

For Those Who Aren't Yet Confident at Parrying

  • Scaling: Vitality & Agility
  • Element: Light
  • Seeram

Level 4

+1 to all Perfection gain, but can't reach Rank S.

Level 10

Base Attack gives 4 Perfection.

Level 20

20% increased Light damage with Skills.

Seeram is a great weapon to boost players' speed in reaching Perfection Ranks, but it limits their ability to utilize the most powerful Rank S moves. As such, the weapon is easy to recommend for players who struggle to execute parries or avoid damage with Verso. With Seeram equipped, players will reach Rank A much faster, either through skills or the base attack. With these tactics, the Combo Attack Pictos or Luminas are invaluable, as it's possible to reach Rank A with a single base attack at the beginning of each round.

Level 20 Seeram slightly compensates for its major downside with increased Light damage from Verso's skills, which helps balance the weapon's damage output without reaching Rank S. Not to mention, Seeram is easily among the most beautiful weapons in the game.

16 Sireso

Capable of Turning Verso Into a Support Character

  • Scaling: Vitality & Agility
  • Element: Physical
  • Sireso

Level 4

Bonus damage from Perfection applies to all allies at half value. Bonus damage no longer applies to Verso.

Level 10

Perfection gained is increased by 1 while Powerful.

Level 20

Support Skills cost 1 less AP.

Sireso definitely won't suit every player, given how strong Verso typically is as a damage dealer, but this weapon is very unique, allowing players to try something different. Sireso's Level 4 perk is literally a game-changer, turning Verso into a support character and granting his bonus Perfection damage to other characters at half value. The weapon's support potential is further boosted at Level 20, making certain skills more affordable to use.

It's easy to see how Sireso can be an easy choice for players most interested in creating devastating character builds for Maelle or Sciel, boosting their damage to unhealthy numbers. In this case, Verso equipped with Sireso becomes a buff machine that grants a substantial damage boost, letting players experiment further with one-shot builds and breeze through even the toughest combat encounters in Expedition 33 .

15 Tireso

Mark, Debuff, and Execute

  • Scaling: Vitality & Defense
  • Element: Earth
  • Tireso

Level 4

Gain 1 Rank by applying Mark.

Level 10

Mark an enemy on Base Attack.

Level 20

Apply Powerless on Marking an enemy.

Players will either love or hate Tireso's weapon design in Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 . However, despite its unserious appearance, Tireso is a surprisingly powerful weapon choice for Verso, with simple yet effective bonuses and synergies. The weapon unleashes the full potential of Marks. Given that Marking is easily achievable through various methods such as Pictos, players can both strengthen their allies and weaken their foes in combat.

With Tireso, Verso reaches Ranks faster, debuffs enemies to make them deal less damage to allies (which works especially well against strong bosses), and sets foes up to be finished off by the character whose turn is next. Given how many of Expedition 33 's characters' skills and attacks benefit from Marks, playing with Tireso can become a secret ingredient for serious boss encounters.

14 Gesam

Some Restrictions Can Be Advantages

  • Scaling: Vitality & Agility
  • Element: Physical
  • Gesam

Level 4

Convert Light damage Skills to Physical damage.

Level 10

20% increased Physical damage with Skills.

Level 20

-1 AP cost for Physical Skills.

While Gesam is a strong choice for Verso, the weapon's primary Level 4 perk is somewhat situational and can limit players when facing bosses that are resistant to Physical damage. Trading the unique Light damage from Verso's skills for the more conventional Physical type won't suit every combat build, yet some players can capitalize on this drastic shift with the right combination of skills and Pictos.

What makes Gesam a powerful weapon is that, along with converting Light damage to Physical, it also increases damage and reduces the AP cost of each converted skill. With such a nice synergy packed into a single weapon, players can overwhelm late-game enemies resistant to Light damage by unleashing high-powered skills more frequently with lower AP demands.

13 Gaulteram

Ol' Reliable Weapon with No Min-Maxing

  • Scaling: Ability & Luck
  • Element: Earth
  • Gaulteram

Level 4

When hit, lose 1 Perfection instead of 1 rank.

Level 10

Apply Rush on Rank S.

Level 20

Gain 2 Perfection on turn start.

Gaulteram hardly makes a case for the strongest Verso or Gustave weapon, yet it's what you might call a reliable workhorse for a large portion of the game, given how early it can be obtained and how useful it can be. Suitable for early- to mid-game, where players are still learning, Gaulteram's primary perk makes mastering Perfection mechanics as Verso much more forgiving, given that getting hit is almost negligible with Gaulteram when it comes to maintaining higher Ranks or their quick recovery.

Even in late game, when some weapons outperform Gaulteram with specific combat builds, some players might prefer sticking with Gaulteram when encountering new enemies in Clair Obscur — just to stay at Rank S most of the time without worrying too much about taking damage from them. Oh, it also looks really cool, boasting a great overall design.

12 Abysseram

For Those Who Aren't Dante

  • Scaling: Vitality & Defense
  • Element: Physical
  • Abysseram

Level 4

50% increased damage on Rank D. No damage increase on other ranks.

Level 10

50% increased Base Attack damage.

Level 20

On Rank D, recover 20% Health with Base Attack.

Abysseram is like the opposite of Chevalam — an OP tool for players who struggle with the game or simply want to try something entirely new with Verso. Equipping Abysseram restricts Verso's combat mechanics quite substantially, but players don't need to worry about gaining ranks higher than D or avoiding damage while building their character around this weapon.

Instead, they receive a flat 50% increase to all damage, an additional 50% increase to base attack damage, and 20% lifeleech if they stay at Rank D, which also means taking hits to remain there. It creates a twisted wheel of life, where Verso is caught in a perpetual cycle of being damaged and healing to sustain both the lower rank and the damage bonus. For some players, it's absolutely worth it.

11 Dreameso

A Mark of a True Professional

  • Scaling: Agility & Luck
  • Element: Physical
  • Dreameso

Level 4

Gain 1 Rank on Counterattack.

Level 10

50% increased Counterattack damage.

Level 20

Gain 2 AP on Counterattack.

If Counterattacks are really your thing in Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 , then Dreameso can be a natural fit — especially during the game's Act 3, when players have already lifted the damage cap to capitalize on this weapon's potential. Naturally, it works best in the hands of seasoned players who know their enemies perfectly, given how tricky it can be to steadily use Counters against enemies with long combo-attack chains that require parrying every incoming attack.

Once parries are mastered, though, Dreameso truly shines, granting extra Rank to Verso with each successful Counterattack while notably boosting Counter damage and granting him additional AP. Even if an enemy is strong enough to survive an enhanced Counter (with bonuses from pictos it could be impossible), it will probably be demolished soon after, during Verso's next turn. On top of that, Dreameso looks really appealing with its exotic design, making players strong and stylish at the same time.

10 Delaram

Easy and Strong Start With Minor Drawback

  • Scaling: Vitality & Luck
  • Element: Light
  • Delaram

Level 4

Start battle on Rank B, but 50% Health.

Level 10

Recover 15% Health on Base Attack.

Level 20

Apply Powerful on Rank B.

Delaram can make playing as Verso much easier, essentially skipping C and D ranks, increasing his damage output, granting him a Powerful buff (additional damage boost), and unlocking extra effects on his skills from the get-go. As a drawback, players start with half HP for Verso, which isn't too big a deal if players are good at the game or have a powerful supporter or healer in their party.

To further amplify Delaram's potential, using Base Attack with this weapon heals for 15% health. Couple this with Combo Attack pictos that add extra strikes to Base Attack, and players can easily recover all their lost HP with a single Base Attack while also keeping to climb Perfection ranks for better results. It's best to use the First Strike picto, though, to ensure Verso always gets the first turn in a fight to possibly eliminate one enemy while recovering his own health.

9 Confuso

Burn 'Em Hard

  • Scaling: Agility & Luck
  • Element: Light
  • Confuso

Level 4

Light damage can Burn on Critical hits.

Level 10

Apply 3 Burn instead of Mark.

Level 20

Increase Burn damage by 50% per Rank, up to 300% on Rank S.

Confuso stands among the stronger options for Verso's primary weapon, especially if players invest in Luck and Critical Hit Chance from Pictos or Luminas equipped, aiming to reach close to 100% Critical Hit value in the mid-game. With that setup, almost every Verso skill that deals Light damage, as well as the base attack, will apply Burn to enemies. Coupled with the character's numerous multi-hit skills, this can be very effective.

Confuso goes further by also allowing players to apply 3 Burn instead of Mark, and increasing Burn damage by up to 300% on Level 20 at Rank S. Not only does this make Burn a secret technique in Verso's arsenal, but using Confuso in Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 is also recommended alongside Maelle in combat. This boosts her Fire skills, which can greatly benefit from Burn stacked on enemies.

What's That Weapon?

Identify the silhouettes before time runs out.

5.0 s
0 / 10

Results

0

High Score: 0

—

More Games
What's That Weapon? Identify the silhouettes before time runs out.
Easy (7.5s)Medium (5.0s)Hard (2.5s)Permadeath (2.5s)
5.0 s
0 / 10
Results
0
High Score: 0 —
More Games

8 Blodam

Unleash Verso's Hidden Nature as a Berserk

  • Scaling: Defense & Agility
  • Element: Light
  • Blodam

Level 4

Perfection is now based on current Health. Gain 1 Rank every 20% missing Health.

Level 10

20% increased Light damage with Skills.

Level 20

+1 AP on Rank Up.

Blodam is Verso's weapon that can truly shine in the right hands, standing as one of the character's strongest weapons in Expedition 33 in terms of raw damage output. Given Blodam's unique perk, it requires Verso to be in battle with no more than 20% HP to instantly reach rank S and gain extra AP, which can be achieved through various pictos or even skills.

It's a risky strategy, but undeniably a rewarding one, provided you can keep Verso alive with all that missing Health and avoid further damage, since you can't really rely on healing with Blodam. There are also some pretty strong synergies with glass cannon pictos in the game that require you to have even less Health (10%) to activate, like At Death's Door, which pairs well with this weapon. The final cherry on top is the increased Light damage with Skills and attacks, since Blodam is a Light weapon, which isn't game-changing, but good to have nevertheless.
item.content:encoded:
In Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 , players can obtain dozens of powerful weapons for each of the main characters. These weapons all have their own types of damage, scaling, as well as unique perks granted upon reaching level 4, level 10, and level 20 with each weapon. The game's unique approach to narrative allows players to access similar weapons via either Gustave or Verso, so both characters essentially share the same arsenal.

However, Verso offers his own unique gameplay style, somewhat similar to Devil May Cry games. The power of the character's skills and attacks grows as Verso reaches Perfection Ranks (from D to S) by attacking enemies and avoiding damage. When choosing the best weapon for Verso in Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 , players have to keep in mind this one-of-a-kind dynamic. Reaching higher tiers like A or S drastically increases all of Verso's damage (up to +200%) while also unlocking additional bonuses for Verso's skills. These are the best weapons for Verso in Expedition 33 .

Updated June 12, 2026, by Vlad Mazanko: With its robust and flexible combat system, rich itemization, and wealth of stats and perks that contribute to success in battle, Clair Obscur is a prime example of an RPG that rewards experimentation, creativity, and skill. Every player can easily find a primary weapon for Verso among the 29 total weapons available that complements their unique playstyle or very specific combat build, unlocking strong synergies with his skills and Perfection mechanics. The weapon's support potential is further boosted at Level 20, making certain skills more affordable to use.

It's easy to see how Sireso can be an easy choice for players most interested in creating devastating character builds for Maelle or Sciel, boosting their damage to unhealthy numbers. In this case, Verso equipped with Sireso becomes a buff machine that grants a substantial damage boost, letting players experiment further with one-shot builds and breeze through even the toughest combat encounters in Expedition 33 .

15 Tireso

  • Scaling: Vitality & Defense
  • Element: Earth
  • Tireso

Level 4

Level 10

Mark an enemy on Base Attack.

Level 20

Apply Powerless on Marking an enemy.

Players will either love or hate Tireso's weapon design in Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 . However, despite its unserious appearance, Tireso is a surprisingly powerful weapon choice for Verso, with simple yet effective bonuses and synergies. The weapon unleashes the full potential of Marks. Given that Marking is easily achievable through various methods such as Pictos, players can both strengthen their allies and weaken their foes in combat.

With Tireso, Verso reaches Ranks faster, debuffs enemies to make them deal less damage to allies (which works especially well against strong bosses), and sets foes up to be finished off by the character whose turn is next. 14 Gesam

Some Restrictions Can Be Advantages

  • Scaling: Vitality & Agility
  • Element: Physical
  • Gesam

Level 10

20% increased Physical damage with Skills.

Level 20

-1 AP cost for Physical Skills.

Trading the unique Light damage from Verso's skills for the more conventional Physical type won't suit every combat build, yet some players can capitalize on this drastic shift with the right combination of skills and Pictos.

13 Gaulteram

Ol' Reliable Weapon with No Min-Maxing

  • Scaling: Ability & Luck
  • Element: Earth
  • Gaulteram

Level 10

Level 20

Oh, it also looks really cool, boasting a great overall design.

12 Abysseram

For Those Who Aren't Dante

  • Scaling: Vitality & Defense
  • Element: Physical
  • Abysseram

Level 4

50% increased damage on Rank D. No damage increase on other ranks.

Level 10

50% increased Base Attack damage.

Level 20

On Rank D, recover 20% Health with Base Attack.

Abysseram is like the opposite of Chevalam — an OP tool for players who struggle with the game or simply want to try something entirely new with Verso. Equipping Abysseram restricts Verso's combat mechanics quite substantially, but players don't need to worry about gaining ranks higher than D or avoiding damage while building their character around this weapon.

Instead, they receive a flat 50% increase to all damage, an additional 50% increase to base attack damage, and 20% lifeleech if they stay at Rank D, which also means taking hits to remain there. It creates a twisted wheel of life, where Verso is caught in a perpetual cycle of being damaged and healing to sustain both the lower rank and the damage bonus. For some players, it's absolutely worth it.

11 Dreameso

A Mark of a True Professional

  • Scaling: Agility & Luck
  • Element: Physical
  • Dreameso

Level 4

Gain 1 Rank on Counterattack.

Level 10

50% increased Counterattack damage.

Level 20

Gain 2 AP on Counterattack.

If Counterattacks are really your thing in Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 , then Dreameso can be a natural fit — especially during the game's Act 3, when players have already lifted the damage cap to capitalize on this weapon's potential. Naturally, it works best in the hands of seasoned players who know their enemies perfectly, given how tricky it can be to steadily use Counters against enemies with long combo-attack chains that require parrying every incoming attack.

Once parries are mastered, though, Dreameso truly shines, granting extra Rank to Verso with each successful Counterattack while notably boosting Counter damage and granting him additional AP. Even if an enemy is strong enough to survive an enhanced Counter (with bonuses from pictos it could be impossible), it will probably be demolished soon after, during Verso's next turn. On top of that, Dreameso looks really appealing with its exotic design, making players strong and stylish at the same time.

10 Delaram

Easy and Strong Start With Minor Drawback

  • Scaling: Vitality & Luck
  • Element: Light
  • Delaram

Level 4

Start battle on Rank B, but 50% Health.

Level 10

Recover 15% Health on Base Attack.

Level 20

Apply Powerful on Rank B.

Delaram can make playing as Verso much easier, essentially skipping C and D ranks, increasing his damage output, granting him a Powerful buff (additional damage boost), and unlocking extra effects on his skills from the get-go. As a drawback, players start with half HP for Verso, which isn't too big a deal if players are good at the game or have a powerful supporter or healer in their party.

To further amplify Delaram's potential, using Base Attack with this weapon heals for 15% health. Couple this with Combo Attack pictos that add extra strikes to Base Attack, and players can easily recover all their lost HP with a single Base Attack while also keeping to climb Perfection ranks for better results. It's best to use the First Strike picto, though, to ensure Verso always gets the first turn in a fight to possibly eliminate one enemy while recovering his own health.

9 Confuso

Burn 'Em Hard

  • Scaling: Agility & Luck
  • Element: Light
  • Confuso

Level 4

Light damage can Burn on Critical hits.

Level 10

Apply 3 Burn instead of Mark.

Level 20

Increase Burn damage by 50% per Rank, up to 300% on Rank S.

Confuso stands among the stronger options for Verso's primary weapon, especially if players invest in Luck and Critical Hit Chance from Pictos or Luminas equipped, aiming to reach close to 100% Critical Hit value in the mid-game. With that setup, almost every Verso skill that deals Light damage, as well as the base attack, will apply Burn to enemies. Coupled with the character's numerous multi-hit skills, this can be very effective.

Confuso goes further by also allowing players to apply 3 Burn instead of Mark, and increasing Burn damage by up to 300% on Level 20 at Rank S. Not only does this make Burn a secret technique in Verso's arsenal, but using Confuso in Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 is also recommended alongside Maelle in combat. This boosts her Fire skills, which can greatly benefit from Burn stacked on enemies.

What's That Weapon?

Identify the silhouettes before time runs out.

5.0 s
0 / 10

Results

0

High Score: 0

—

More Games
What's That Weapon? Identify the silhouettes before time runs out.
Easy (7.5s)Medium (5.0s)Hard (2.5s)Permadeath (2.5s)
5.0 s
0 / 10
Results
0
High Score: 0 —
More Games

8 Blodam

Unleash Verso's Hidden Nature as a Berserk

  • Scaling: Defense & Agility
  • Element: Light
  • Blodam

Level 4

Perfection is now based on current Health. Gain 1 Rank every 20% missing Health.

Level 10

20% increased Light damage with Skills.

Level 20

+1 AP on Rank Up.

Blodam is Verso's weapon that can truly shine in the right hands, standing as one of the character's strongest weapons in Expedition 33 in terms of raw damage output. Given Blodam's unique perk, it requires Verso to be in battle with no more than 20% HP to instantly reach rank S and gain extra AP, which can be achieved through various pictos or even skills.

It's a risky strategy, but undeniably a rewarding one, provided you can keep Verso alive with all that missing Health and avoid further damage, since you can't really rely on healing with Blodam. There are also some pretty strong synergies with glass cannon pictos in the game that require you to have even less Health (10%) to activate, like At Death's Door, which pairs well with this weapon. The final cherry on top is the increased Light damage with Skills and attacks, since Blodam is a Light weapon, which isn't game-changing, but good to have nevertheless.

item.title: Blasphemous 2: All Altarpiece Locations
item.description:
The Altarpieces , also known as Figures, can be equipped at a Prie Dieu or Shrine in Blasphemous 2 . Each one provides a passive effect that can make exploration, platforming, and boss fights considerably easier.

The Third Sin DLC has added several new Altarpieces to the game, some of which directly enhance the capabilities of the new weapon, Embrujo . If you're looking to collect every Figure and expand your build options, the guide below covers all of their locations.

All Altarpiece Locations in Blasphemous 2

Eliseo

  • Effect : Increases base Embrujo damage.

From the Prie Dieu located in the middle of Stone Entrails , head left into the next room. In this room, drop down and make your way to the far left side, where you can hit the wall to reveal a hidden passage. Here, you will encounter several enemies. Defeating them allows you to open the chest and obtain the Eliseo figure.

The Perfidy

  • Effect : The effectiveness of the servant accompanying the Penitent One increases according to the Fervour accumulated by the latter. The greater the Fervour, the greater the promptness of the servant.

Go back to the same shrine as the previous collectible, head down the ladder on the right, and enter the next room. Drop down and go right to find a Red Crest Gate that can only be opened with a Ring obtained through progression in the DLC. Go past it, grapple upward, and make your way to the next room. Here, you will need to complete a Bell section to reach the chest containing the altarpiece.

The Grief

  • Effect : Grants invulnerability while using Bile Flasks.

From the eastern shrine in Stone Entrails, head right and drop down to land on the platform just before the spikes. Hit the wall on the right to reveal a hidden passage. Then make your way up to find this altarpiece on the platform to the left.

Lady of the Mist

  • Effect : Defeating enemies gradually increases the damage dealt by all weapons up to a maximum level. This bonus is completely lost upon death, taking damage, or resting at a Prie Dieu.

Go right from the Usurped Halls Prie Dieu until you exit the room. In the next room, keep heading upward and take the first path on the left to find the chest.

Crescencia

  • Effect: Servants inflict contact damage on enemies.

From the western shrine in Geometry of Knowledge , head left and then make your way all the way up in the next room to exit through the left side. Continue left and, when you reach the vertical section of The Vibrating of the Organs , head down and exit through the passage on the left. You will spot a chain hanging down. Jump and try to land on it, then grab the item to obtain the figure.

The Ascension

  • Effect : Performing Passage of Ash causes the Penitent One to release a shockwave that damages enemies.

Fast travel to the middle Prie Dieu in Geometry of Knowledge, climb the ladder, and exit into the room on the right. On the right side of this room is a movable shelf that functions as a ladder. Hit it until it reaches the far left corner. You can then climb it and interact with the chest above.

The Judicious One

  • Effect : Increases Embrujo Unholy damage.

From the shrine in Usurped Halls, head all the way to the right until you reach a Red Crest Gate. Continue exploring the area and keep climbing upward. Eventually, you will reach a room in The Vibrating of the Organs where you can head left to spot a switch above you. Activating it enables two grapple points that can be used to reach the altarpiece.

Inner Voice

  • Effect : Wail of the Flame uses less of the gauge's power.

Head left from the western Prie Dieu in The Vibrating of the Organs. Grapple across to the other side and enter the next room. Start heading down and to the right. When you reach the ladder with the shortcut switch, look slightly above you to spot a grapple point leading to the chest.

Cartography of the Bones

  • Effect ​​​​​​: Enemy attacks that would normally knock down the Penitent One now cause knockback instead.

From the shrine in the middle of The Vibrating of the Organs, where you obtain the Ring that opens the Red Crest Gates, head all the way right until you reach the elevator room. Call the elevator up and jump on top of it to find the chest containing the final altarpiece.

More Blasphemous 2 Guides

If you need help with some of the Blasphemous 2 guides, make sure to check these out:

  • All Bosses in The Third Sin DLC
  • All Skin Locations

Blasphemous 2

Follow
Followed
Soulslike
Metroidvania
Platformer
Systems
OpenCritic Reviews
Top Critic Avg: 84 /100 Critics Rec: 93%
Released
August 24, 2023
ESRB
M For Mature 17+ // Blood and Gore, Partial Nudity, Violence
Developer(s)
The Game Kitchen
Publisher(s)
Team17

WHERE TO PLAY

SUBSCRIPTION
DIGITAL
FX Wiki Wiki

Genre(s)
Soulslike, Metroidvania, Platformer
Powered by
item.content:encoded:
The Altarpieces , also known as Figures, can be equipped at a Prie Dieu or Shrine in Blasphemous 2 . Each one provides a passive effect that can make exploration, platforming, and boss fights considerably easier.

The Third Sin DLC has added several new Altarpieces to the game, some of which directly enhance the capabilities of the new weapon, Embrujo . If you're looking to collect every Figure and expand your build options, the guide below covers all of their locations.

All Altarpiece Locations in Blasphemous 2

Eliseo

  • Effect : Increases base Embrujo damage.

From the Prie Dieu located in the middle of Stone Entrails , head left into the next room. In this room, drop down and make your way to the far left side, where you can hit the wall to reveal a hidden passage. Here, you will encounter several enemies. Defeating them allows you to open the chest and obtain the Eliseo figure.

The Perfidy

  • Effect : The effectiveness of the servant accompanying the Penitent One increases according to the Fervour accumulated by the latter. The greater the Fervour, the greater the promptness of the servant.

Go back to the same shrine as the previous collectible, head down the ladder on the right, and enter the next room. Drop down and go right to find a Red Crest Gate that can only be opened with a Ring obtained through progression in the DLC. Go past it, grapple upward, and make your way to the next room. Here, you will need to complete a Bell section to reach the chest containing the altarpiece.

The Grief

  • Effect : Grants invulnerability while using Bile Flasks.

From the eastern shrine in Stone Entrails, head right and drop down to land on the platform just before the spikes. Hit the wall on the right to reveal a hidden passage. Then make your way up to find this altarpiece on the platform to the left.

Lady of the Mist

  • Effect : Defeating enemies gradually increases the damage dealt by all weapons up to a maximum level. This bonus is completely lost upon death, taking damage, or resting at a Prie Dieu.

Go right from the Usurped Halls Prie Dieu until you exit the room. In the next room, keep heading upward and take the first path on the left to find the chest.

Crescencia

  • Effect: Servants inflict contact damage on enemies.

From the western shrine in Geometry of Knowledge , head left and then make your way all the way up in the next room to exit through the left side. Continue left and, when you reach the vertical section of The Vibrating of the Organs , head down and exit through the passage on the left. You will spot a chain hanging down. Jump and try to land on it, then grab the item to obtain the figure.

The Ascension

  • Effect : Performing Passage of Ash causes the Penitent One to release a shockwave that damages enemies.

Fast travel to the middle Prie Dieu in Geometry of Knowledge, climb the ladder, and exit into the room on the right. On the right side of this room is a movable shelf that functions as a ladder. Hit it until it reaches the far left corner. You can then climb it and interact with the chest above.

The Judicious One

  • Effect : Increases Embrujo Unholy damage.

From the shrine in Usurped Halls, head all the way to the right until you reach a Red Crest Gate. Continue exploring the area and keep climbing upward. Eventually, you will reach a room in The Vibrating of the Organs where you can head left to spot a switch above you. Activating it enables two grapple points that can be used to reach the altarpiece.

Inner Voice

  • Effect : Wail of the Flame uses less of the gauge's power.

Head left from the western Prie Dieu in The Vibrating of the Organs. Grapple across to the other side and enter the next room. Start heading down and to the right. When you reach the ladder with the shortcut switch, look slightly above you to spot a grapple point leading to the chest.

Cartography of the Bones

  • Effect ​​​​​​: Enemy attacks that would normally knock down the Penitent One now cause knockback instead.

From the shrine in the middle of The Vibrating of the Organs, where you obtain the Ring that opens the Red Crest Gates, head all the way right until you reach the elevator room. Call the elevator up and jump on top of it to find the chest containing the final altarpiece.

More Blasphemous 2 Guides

If you need help with some of the Blasphemous 2 guides, make sure to check these out:

  • All Bosses in The Third Sin DLC
  • All Skin Locations
